category.description: Optional category. Use "bug" for incorrect behavior, "usability" for confusing UX, "missing_feature" for capabilities the user wished existed, "documentation" for unclear docs, "performance" for slow responses, "praise" for positive feedback, or "other".

feedback.description: The feedback message to share with PostHog. For user-initiated feedback, capture the user's words faithfully. For agent-observed friction, only call this tool after the user has explicitly agreed. Never include the user's data, query results, credentials, or anything sensitive — summarize instead.

posthog_area.description: Free-text area of PostHog the feedback is about, e.g. "session replay", "feature flags", "insights", "dashboards", "data warehouse", "MCP", "MCP skills", "billing", "docs". Omit if not applicable.

severity.description: Optional severity for issues. "high" = blocked the task, "medium" = painful workaround, "low" = minor polish. Omit for non-issue feedback (praise, ideas).

skill_name.description: The PostHog skill the feedback is about, if any (e.g. "querying-posthog-data"). Omit if not about a skill.

source.description: How the feedback originated. "user_initiated" = the user asked you to send feedback (any topic). "agent_observed" = you noticed friction while working and the user agreed to share it.

tool_name.description: The MCP tool the feedback is about, if any (e.g. "query-run"). Omit if the feedback is not MCP-tool-specific.
